name: Open PR
instructions:
Create a pull request for the implementation.

## Pre-flight Check

Verify the feature branch exists and has commits ahead of main:
```bash
git branch --show-current
# Must be feature/<issue-id>-<slug>, NOT main

git log main..HEAD --oneline
# Must show commits (spec, tests, implementation)
```

If commits are on main instead of a branch, create a branch retroactively:
```bash
# Find where main was before our commits
git log --oneline main | head -10
# Create branch from current HEAD, reset main to before our commits
git checkout -b feature/<issue-id>-<slug>
git push -u origin feature/<issue-id>-<slug>
```

## PR Creation

1. Ensure the feature branch is rebased on latest main with no conflicts:
```bash
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main
git push --force-with-lease origin feature/<issue-id>-<slug>
```

2. Write a concise PR title (< 70 chars, imperative mood)

3. Create the PR with `gh pr create`:
```bash
gh pr create \
  --title "feat: <description>" \
  --body "## Summary\n\n<what changed>\n\n## Test Plan\n\n<how to verify>\n\nImplements: <parent-issue-id>" \
  --base main \
  --head feature/<issue-id>-<slug>
```

4. PR body must include:
   - Summary of changes
   - Test plan
   - Reference to the parent issue
   - Link to the design spec path

Do NOT merge — just open the PR and output 'done'.
